page: Vultr vhf-2c-4gb is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the High Frequency Compute family. It features 2 shared Intel Core Processor (Skylake, IBRS) physical cores operating at 2.0 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 128 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

Benchmark results indicate a mix of average and lower-tier performance. The server achieves average single-core CPU performance in stress-ng (1873.78) and strong single-threaded gzip compression (32.63 GB/sec). However, multi-core CPU performance is weak (3596.49 in stress-ng), and memory write bandwidth is poor at 12.89 GB/sec. Database operations also score poorly on PassMark (999.52), while Redis SET operations reach 2.61 million operations per second, placing it in the bottom 25% of tested hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a low-cost entry point with bundled NVMe storage, though the shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, and development environments rather than heavy database operations or memory-intensive workloads.
description: Vultr vhf-2c-4gb is an x86_64 server in the High Frequency Compute family, configured with 2 shared Intel Skylake physical cores running at 2.0 GHz and 4.0 GB of RAM. It includes a bundled 128 GB NVMe SSD and one public IPv4 address. Performance benchmarks reveal average single-core capabilities, highlighted by strong single-threaded gzip compression at 32.63 GB/sec, but weak multi-core and database performance. Memory write bandwidth is poor at 12.89 GB/sec, and PassMark database operations score in the bottom 10%. Given the shared CPU allocation and modest memory capacity, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic static web serving, and development environments rather than intensive database or multi-threaded workloads.
og_description: Vultr vhf-2c-4gb is an x86_64 server featuring 2 shared Intel Skylake cores, 4.0 GB RAM, and a 128 GB NVMe SSD, offering average single-core performance but weak multi-core and database capabilities.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-2c-4gb is an x86_64 server with 2 shared Intel cores, 4.0 GB RAM, and 128 GB NVMe storage, suited for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-core server combining local NVMe storage with capable single-threaded performance for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared Intel Skylake physical cores running at 2.0 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory and 128 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance of 32.63 GB/sec
Weak multi-core performance and poor memory write bandwidth
Suited for basic web serving, lightweight utility tasks, and development
